sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal. Arab. بَأَرَ to dig, specially a well, a pit. Kindred roots are בּוּר, בָּרַרArab. فأر to dig (rather more remote, בָּרָא, בָּרָה), Lat. forare, Germ. bohren [Engl. to bore]. Comp. בְּאֵר, בֹּאר, בֹּור.

Piel.-

(1) to engrave letters on a stone. Followed by עַל Deu 27:8: Hab 2:2.

(2) to explain, to declare, pr. to dig out the sense, and to set it forth when dug out, Deu 1:5. The derivatives follow immediately, except בֹּור, בֵּרִי, בֵּרֹותָה or בֵּרֹותַי.
